database_info.name: Video Wall Selection Theory - Standards Based
database_info.purpose: Engineering design tool for video wall system selection based on international standards

1_technology_selection_lcd_vs_led.principle: Technology choice is driven by viewing distance, seam tolerance, brightness needs, and budget

comparison.best_for: Control rooms, NOC/SOC, surveillance centers, command centers
comparison.advantages: Lower cost per sqm, higher native resolution per panel, proven 24/7 reliability
comparison.disadvantages: Visible seams between panels, brightness limited vs LED, larger depth

comparison.best_for: Boardrooms, exhibition halls, lobbies, broadcast, high-end command centers
comparison.advantages: Zero seam, higher brightness, longer lifespan, front-serviceable, flexible sizes
comparison.disadvantages: Higher cost per sqm, lower native resolution per module at larger pixel pitches

1_technology_selection_lcd_vs_led.decision_rule: If seam visibility is critical → LED. If budget is primary concern and seams are acceptable → LCD. If viewing distance > 3m and P2.5 LED is used, pixel visibility is not an issue.

2_pixel_pitch_selection_led.principle: Pixel pitch determines minimum comfortable viewing distance

min_viewing_distance_m.source: LED industry standard rule of thumb

3_lcd_bezel_seam_selection.principle: Smaller bezel = less visual disruption but higher cost

options.application: Surveillance video wall, budget control room, general monitoring
Command center, NOC/SOC, executive briefing room

3_lcd_bezel_seam_selection.guideline: For surveillance with 16+ camera channels: 3.5mm seam is acceptable. For map/GIS/SCADA single-image display: 1.8mm or LED recommended.

4_screen_size_and_layout.principle: Total video wall size is driven by viewing distance, content, and operator count

viewing_distance_by_wall.formula: Min viewing distance = 1.5 × diagonal of total wall
viewing_distance_by_wall.note: For LED walls, also apply pixel pitch minimum distance

5_brightness_requirements.principle: Video wall brightness must overcome ambient light and maintain uniformity

requirements_by_environment.note: Lower brightness reduces eye strain in dark rooms
Brighter needed to overcome ambient reflections

uniformity.lcd_requirement: Factory calibrated color and brightness uniformity across all panels
uniformity.note: Hikvision LCD video walls include factory calibration for multi-panel uniformity

6_controller_sizing.principle: Controller must support total pixel load and input requirements
6_controller_sizing.lcd_controller_note: LCD video walls use built-in scalers per panel + optional external video wall processor for source management. Each LCD panel has its own HDMI/DVI/DP/VGA inputs and HDMI loop-out for daisy chain up to 30 screens.

7_structural_mounting.principle: Wall structure must support total weight with safety margin

lcd_mounting.wall_requirement: Reinforced concrete or steel stud wall
lcd_mounting.alignment: Brackets provide fine adjustment for bezel alignment (critical for 1.8mm seam)

led_mounting.bracket_material: AL6063 Aluminum with electrostatic spray coating
led_mounting.front_access: LED modules are front-serviceable — no rear access needed

7_structural_mounting.seismic_note: In seismic zones (IBC/ASCE 7), video wall mounting must include anti-tip safety restraints

8_power_infrastructure.principle: Dedicated circuits with UPS for video wall systems

lcd_power_sizing.ups_recommendation: Online UPS, minimum 15 min runtime, sized for total load + 20%

led_power_sizing.note: Size circuit breakers for peak consumption, UPS for average

heat_dissipation.note: Add to room HVAC load calculation. Video walls are significant heat sources.

9_signal_distribution.principle: Signal path must support resolution at required distance without degradation

lcd_signal_options.note: Built into Hikvision LCD panels. Simplest cabling.
Required for complex layouts and windowing
Uses Cat6a cabling. Best for long runs.
For surveillance-only video walls via iVMS/HikCentral

led_signal.method: DS-D42C08-H controller → Gigabit Ethernet → LED modules

10_environmental_and_ventilation.principle: Video walls generate significant heat and require planned ventilation

lcd_rear_clearance_mm.note: For passive cooling and maintenance access

led_rear_clearance_mm.note: LED front-serviceable requires less rear space

requirements.airflow: Ensure air circulation behind LCD panels. Avoid enclosed cavities without ventilation.
requirements.hvac_load: Add full video wall power consumption to room cooling calculation

11_compliance_standards.principle: All video wall components must meet applicable standards

video_wall_specific.scope: CCTV monitor requirements for surveillance video walls
Control centre ergonomic design — display layout, viewing distances, operator stations
